title: ferray-fft: all FFT functions flatten input via iter().copied().collect() before computing
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Every FFT function starts with 'let data = to_complex_flat(a)' which does a.iter().copied().collect() — allocating a full copy of the input. For a contiguous array, the data is already in memory in the right order. NumPy's pocketfft operates on the array's buffer directly via strides. For contiguous inputs, ferray should pass as_slice() directly to rustfft instead of copying. For non-contiguous inputs, the lane extraction in nd.rs already handles the strided access pattern, so the initial flatten is doubly wasteful.
